commit.message:
fix: keep csr_if.rsp_ready=1 to prevent decoder ST_HOLD_RSP deadlock

The CSR decoder FSM goes ST_IDLE → ST_HOLD_RSP on every write,
asserting rsp_valid and waiting for rsp_ready to return to IDLE.
The BFM's csr_write task never drove rsp_ready (init 0), so the
decoder got stuck after the very first write — req_ready stayed
low and all subsequent transactions hung.

Fix: initialise rsp_ready=1 (always high) so the decoder can
always retire responses immediately. Removed the toggle logic
in csr_read since rsp_ready no longer needs gating.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
perf(bench): unified 3-phase Maglev warmup for all precompiled benchmarks

Refactored all 9 precompiled benchmarks to use warmup_with_maglev() helper:
- Phase 1: 100 interpreter iterations to warm ICs + trigger Maglev
- Phase 2: Reset deopt counter + 100 JIT validation iterations (warms JIT ICs)
- Phase 3: Final deopt reset before Criterion measurement

Also capped deopt backoff at 2^10 (1024) instead of 2^20 (1M) to allow
recovery from transient deopts like cold JIT ICs after compilation.

This ensures Maglev is properly warmed AND its inline caches populated
before benchmarking, giving representative JIT performance numbers.

commit.message:
Reject banned inbound peers at HELLO before completing handshake

Move ban check into Peer::accept() handshake, immediately after recv_hello()
and before sending any response. Mirrors stellar-core's Peer::recvHello()
which checks isBanned() before AUTH. The post-handshake check in
handle_accepted_inbound_peer remains as defense-in-depth.

Fixes #1150

* Add MotorController module with IMU-aided driving

New motor_controller.py module providing high-level async motor control:
- Accelerometer-based distance driving (forward_mm/backward_mm) using
  double-integration with proportional deceleration for accurate stopping
- Gyro-based precise turning (turn/turn_left/turn_right) using IMU feedback
- Time-based driving and turning (forward/backward/timed_turn) preserved
- Acceleration ramps for smooth start/stop
- front_face orientation support: rotates accelerometer X/Y axes to match
  actual robot forward direction regardless of HexDrive mounting position
- Diagnostic output ([MC-DIAG]) for calibration and debugging
- run_instructions() translates button sequences respecting drive_mode setting

app.py changes:
- Import and create MotorController when HexDrive is detected
- Wire async instruction execution via _run_instructions_async()
- Add settings: drive_mode (Time/Distance), drive_step_mm, accel_scale

constants.py changes:
- Add drive mode, distance step, and accelerometer tuning constants

Note: Gyro-based turning has not yet been tuned/calibrated to the same
degree as accelerometer distance driving. Distance mode achieves ~98mm
accuracy on 100mm targets after calibration.

- Implement full PID algorithm with proportional, integral, and derivative terms
- Add diagnostic logging output showing error, P/I/D terms, correction and output
- Reset PID state when exiting follower mode

commit.message:
feat: auto-enable upgrade-node and reinstall-openclaw for latest version

When install-method=nvm and version config is "" or "latest", running
'juju run <app>/leader upgrade' now automatically upgrades Node.js and
reinstalls OpenClaw without needing to pass upgrade-node=true and
reinstall-openclaw=true explicitly.

Implementation:
- Change upgrade-node and reinstall-openclaw param types from boolean
  to string with default "auto" in actions.yaml. This allows the action
  script to distinguish between "user said nothing" (auto) vs an
  explicit "true" or "false" override — something impossible with
  boolean defaults since action-get always returns the declared default.
- In actions/upgrade: resolve "auto" at runtime by checking the
  version and install-method configs. Both params default to true when
  install-method!=bun and version is "" or "latest", false otherwise.
  Explicit "true"/"false" values always take effect as-is.

Verified: 'juju run linebot/leader upgrade skip-reboot=true' (no other
params) logs 'reinstall-openclaw=true, upgrade-node=true' and performs
the full Node.js upgrade + OpenClaw reinstall automatically.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(bench): remove per-iteration deopt reset that caused 250x regression

The per-iteration ba.reset_maglev_deopt_count() inside b.iter() forced
Maglev to retry on every Criterion iteration even when it consistently
deopts. For deep_object this caused tried=3312 deopted=3312 (all
overflow), adding ~0.5us deopt overhead per iteration (796us total).
Without the reset, Maglev's exponential backoff kicks in after warmup
deopts, letting the interpreter run at full speed (~3us for deep_object).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Route targeted flood messages through flow control

try_send_to() now checks is_flood_message() and routes flow-controlled
messages (SCP, Transaction, FloodAdvert, FloodDemand) through the Flood
path instead of direct Send, matching stellar-core's Peer::sendMessage()
which always flow-controls flood messages regardless of broadcast vs.
targeted send.

Fixes #1185

Fix class static property ordering: preserve insertion order for length/name

Add clear_attr_markers() helper that removes attribute hidden-keys without
removing the main key from IndexMap. Use it in InitProperty's VmFunction
handler so that IndexMap::insert updates the value in-place, preserving
the original insertion order for built-in keys like 'length' and 'name'
when overridden by static class methods.

Fixes cluster 23: fn-length-static-precedence-order and
fn-name-static-precedence-order tests now pass.

commit.message:
fix(ui): replace double-width icons to fix column alignment

The ⏳ (hourglass) and ⏸ (pause) Unicode characters are double-width,
occupying 2 terminal columns instead of 1. This shifted all columns
right by 1 on Running rows, breaking vertical alignment.

Replace with single-width alternatives:
- ⏳ → ● (black circle) for in-progress builds
- ⏸ → ◆ (black diamond) for pending approvals
